title: lycium intricatum subsp. pujosii
intro: Lycium intricatum subsp. pujosii, commonly known as a sub-species of the Lycium intricatum plant, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the nightshade family, native to the Andean regions of South America. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Lycium intricatum subsp. pujosii include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve immune function, and protect against oxidative stress. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used in traditional medicine to support overall health and well-being.
